claim: Iranian Army spokesperson Mohammad Akrami Nia declared that Iran does not consider the war with the United States and Israel to have ended despite the ceasefire, signaling the conflict is far from over.
Iran has continued updating its target database even during the pause in fighting, with preparations for potential future strikes actively underway.
Israeli airstrikes struck Hezbollah targets deep in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley on April 28, 2026, in response to Hezbollah attacks including a drone strike that hit IDF troops.
The IDF discovered and seized weapons, drones, large mines, and rockets from Hezbollah positions in southern Lebanon.
US-Iran ceasefire negotiations stalled with Iran proposing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while postponing discussion of its nuclear program.
President Trump is reportedly unhappy with Iran's ceasefire framework, viewing the blockade as Washington's strongest leverage.
The White House is weighing whether continued economic pressure is sufficient or if renewed military action will be needed to force Iran into serious concessions.
The US military maintains a blockade on Iranian shipping through the Persian Gulf and has continued its force buildup in the region.
Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps gunboats have fired on container ships and cargo vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
President Trump canceled planned U.S.-Iran peace talks in Pakistan, citing that there was too much time wasted on traveling.
Iran apparently never had any intention of continuing talks because their demands had not been met, according to reporting on the canceled Pakistan talks.
A US logistics and security team was present in Islamabad to facilitate a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran.
Reports indicate Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei may be seriously wounded and injured, though Iran has coordinated efforts to deny claims of leadership divisions.
Iran sent high-ranking officials including Foreign Minister Zarif and parliamentary speaker Qalibaq to Islamabad, signaling Iran's chain of command and leadership structure remain intact.
Internal divisions have emerged in Iran over negotiations with the United States, with Iranian lawmakers and state-linked outlets calling for increased secrecy around negotiations.
Iran sent a negotiating team spanning an unusually broad political spectrum, suggesting a calculated effort to preempt hardline backlash.
Recent protests in Iran marked the most serious domestic unrest the Islamic Republic has ever faced, with potential to fundamentally alter or even topple the regime.

The regime has responded to protests with unprecedented brutality, conducting a brutal crackdown and implementing extended internet blackouts including efforts to confiscate Starlink and satellite communications dishes.
The regime has arrested dozens of security forces in Kermanshah who refused to fire at protesters.
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Intelligence Organization stated it is dealing with possible acts of abandonment, suggesting potential defections among security forces.
Nearly 5,000 Iranian-backed militia fighters reportedly entered Iran from southern Iraq to support the crackdown on protesters.
Iran's political stability index declined to -1.58 points in 2024 on a scale from -2.5 weak to 2.5 strong, down from -1.27 in 2023.
Iran has deeper coordination between its regular army and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps during the conflict, pointing to a more unified military posture.
